questions.q: Emma: Jack, I want to talk to you for a while. I’m feeling really disappointed about not getting the job I interviewed for. Jack: I can understand how you must be feeling sad right now. options: You should have prepared better, Emma.

Maybe you lacked the required skills. III. 綜合測驗 說明:以下兩篇短文,共有 10 個空格,為第 21 至 30 題,每題有四個選項,請依各篇短文文意, 選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 ▲ 下篇短文共有 5 個空格,為第 21-25 題,請依短文文意,選出一個最適合該空格的答案。 Hari Budha Magar, a brave ex-soldier from Nepal, experienced a terrible incident while serving in Afghanistan. He lost both of his legs in a bomb attack. 21 this hardship, however, he didn’t give up on his dreams. In 2023, he did something remarkable—reaching the summit of Mount Everest. The climb began on April 17, 2023, exactly 13 years after he lost his legs. The weather on the mountain was extremely bad. 22, Hari and his team had to wait for clear skies at the base camp for a total of 18 days. They encountered a lot of challenges throughout their journey. Yet, Hari’s strong determination and his team’s help, 23 kept them going, led them to successfully reach the summit of the mountain. This accomplishment made Hari the first person with both legs 24 above the knees to conquer Mount Everest. Hari’s journey serves 25 an inspiration to everyone. It proves that with great effort and a united team, one can overcome difficult circumstances and accomplish amazing goals.

questions.explanation: 根據這張折線圖,下列哪一年的出生人數和死亡人數 (B) 約瑟夫和妹妹各自訂閱《洞察今日臺灣》的數位 差距最大? 高級版。 (A) 2011 。 (B) 2017 。 (C) 2020 。 (D) 2023 。 (C) 約瑟夫訂閱《洞察今日臺灣》的數位基本版,而 說明: 2023 年死亡人數為 20 萬 5 千 3 百 68 人,出 妹妹訂閱數位 & 印刷版本。 生人數為 13 萬 5 千 5 百 71 人,差距為 6 萬 9 千 7 百 (D) 約瑟夫訂閱 《洞察今日臺灣》 的數位 & 印刷版本, 97 人,比其他三年差距更大 並和妹妹分享他的帳戶。

questions.q: If Alex subscribes to the digital premium version of Today’s Taiwan Insight, which of the following features might he be unable to use?

super students ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 38-42 題 Pay toilets have a long history dating back to ancient Roman times, during which individuals would pay to use public restrooms. Even today, pay toilets can be found in various places worldwide. These facilities require a small entry fee, and it helps to ensure their maintenance. Let’s see how different countries or cities deal with the issue of pay toilets. In Taiwan, public restrooms for women at railway and bus stations, as well as in traditional markets, used to charge a fee because they provided toilet paper, while men’s restrooms were free to use. This practice clearly went against sexual equality and was later stopped. Today, most public toilets in Taiwan are free of charge, and an increasing number of them are beginning to offer free toilet paper. In the Czech Republic, restrooms generally charge a fee, and this is also common in subway stations. Besides, some stores print bathroom codes on the receipt. So, if customers shop there, they can use the restroom many times. However, many department stores and large hotels do not charge, and their restrooms can be used when necessary. Pay toilets are often cleaner because they have people who quickly replace toilet paper and clean the restrooms right after they are used. In North America, the concept of pay toilets was accepted to different extents. Cities like New York City and San Francisco experimented with pay toilets in the past, but this concept received varied feedback, leading to public pay toilets being banned in many states. Toronto also adopted pay public toilets to put a damper on individuals using business restrooms without permission. This decision followed complaints from businesses about non-customers using their facilities without buying anything. In conclusion, pay toilets have experienced significant changes over time and are still found in various locations worldwide. Currently, different countries are striving to maintain public restrooms and discourage misuse.

tolerate 二、非選擇題 ( 第 I 題,每格 2 分,共 4 分;第 II 題 6 分;第 III 題 6 分;共 16 分 ) I. 填充 說明: ▲請依據中文提示,將試題內空格  、  答案之完整單字 ( 含提示之字首 ),分別作答於答 案卷之作答欄  、  之指定範圍內以完成句子。 ▲請勿抄題,每格限填一字,超過一字者視為錯誤,不予計分。
questions.explanation: 以下何者與第四段中的「 put a damper on 」語意最相 筆費用,這有助於確保它們的維護。讓我們看看不同的 近? 國家或城市是如何處理付費廁所的問題。 (A) 阻止 (B) 支配 (C) 說服 (D) 容忍 在臺灣,鐵路和公車站以及傳統市場的女性公廁過 去曾經要收費,因為它們提供了衛生紙,而男性廁所則 二、非選擇題 免費使用。這種做法明顯違反了性別平等原則,後來被 停止了。今天,臺灣大部分的公共廁所都免費,而且越 I. 填充 來越多的公共廁所開始提供免費衛生紙。
